steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content:
Left of blank: subject “Lucassie Koperqualuk's guitar riffs” with adverb modifier “effortlessly” — part of an incomplete independent clause
Right of blank: noun phrase “Charlie Adams's delightfully catchy vocal melodies” — object

steps.title: Apply the rule for connecting verbs to prepositions
steps.content:
The rule is that a verb and the preposition immediately following it cannot be separated by punctuation, which “Blend with” correctly observes. Options A, B, and C each incorrectly insert punctuation (comma, period, or semicolon) between the verb and preposition.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
